task: Write a Python function collapse_delims(s) that splits the string on commas and semicolons, treats any run of consecutive delimiters, mixed or not, as one split, drops empty pieces at the start and end, and returns the pieces rejoined with a single comma between each. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the criteria, with a well-structured approach to handling delimiters and edge cases.
trace: The function `collapse_delims(s)` takes a string s as input, removes leading and trailing spaces using the strip() method. It then uses list comprehension to split the string using either commas or semicolons as delimiters, and drops any empty pieces created in the process. To handle an edge case where the last piece ends with a delimiter, we remove that delimiter from the last piece before joining the list back into a single string using join().
